title: Velvety Dutch Red Potatoes Stir-fry
description: An earthy and velvety one-pot dutch dish featuring red potatoes that have been simmered to a smooth texture, served as a high-protein-friendly side or main course.
instructions: Peel and dice the red potatoes into bite-sized pieces. Dice the onion, garlic, and celery.
In a large pot or Dutch oven, combine potatoes, diced onions, garlic, and about half of the olive oil. Season with salt and pepper.
Add enough water to cover the ingredients by 1 inch. Bring to a boil then reduce heat to medium-low, stirring occasionally until the mixture reduces slightly (about 30 minutes).
During the last 5-7 minutes of cooking, add celery and bell pepper. Increase heat to high, adding more olive oil if necessary.
Taste for seasoning; adjust salt or pepper as needed. Stir in baking soda at this point. Continue simmering until the potatoes are tender but not falling apart.
Serve hot with a drizzle of extra olive oil and chopped parsley. Can be served with grilled chicken or steak for added protein.
